top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Başkent's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Gran Fondo Başkent's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
Gran Fondo Başkent's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Base fitness for Gran Fondo Başkent. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Başkent. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Gran Fondo Başkent's mixed terrain actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Başkent, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
At Gran Fondo Başkent, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Başkent’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Başkent. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Ankara, Turkey.
